subject: Biology
chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production

definitions.definition: A period of rapid increase in agricultural production in developing countries, initiated by the introduction of high-yielding varieties of crops, irrigation, fertilizers, pesticides and modern agricultural technology.
definitions.example: The Green Revolution was first initiated in India in the 1960s by M.S. Swaminathan.

definitions.definition: A crop variety that produces a significantly higher yield than the local varieties, often through the use of modern agricultural technology and inputs.
definitions.example: HYVs of wheat, rice, and maize were introduced in India during the Green Revolution.

definitions.definition: Seeds produced by crossing two different crop varieties to produce offspring with desirable traits.
definitions.example: Hybrid seeds of maize and wheat are widely used in agriculture.

definitions.definition: The direct manipulation of an organism's genes using biotechnology to introduce desirable traits.
definitions.example: Genetic engineering is used to produce genetically modified crops with improved yield and disease resistance.

definitions.definition: The use of biological systems, living organisms, or derivatives thereof, to develop new products, technologies, and processes.
definitions.example: Biotechnology is used in agriculture to produce genetically modified crops.

definitions.definition: A method of irrigation that delivers water directly to the roots of plants, reducing water loss and increasing water efficiency.
definitions.example: Micro-irrigation is used in many parts of the world to conserve water and reduce evaporation.

definitions.definition: A type of micro-irrigation that delivers water directly to the base of plants, reducing evaporation and runoff.
definitions.example: Drip irrigation is widely used in agriculture to conserve water and reduce labor costs.

definitions.definition: A method of irrigation that uses sprinklers to distribute water over a large area.
definitions.example: Sprinkler irrigation is commonly used in agriculture to water large fields.

definitions.definition: A farming approach that uses advanced technology and data analysis to optimize crop yields and reduce waste.
definitions.example: Precision farming uses GPS, drones, and satellite imaging to monitor crop health and optimize irrigation.

definitions.definition: The practice of growing a variety of crops on the same land to reduce dependence on a single crop and increase overall productivity.
definitions.example: Crop diversification is used in many parts of the world to reduce the risk of crop failure and increase biodiversity.

definitions.definition: A holistic approach to managing pests that combines physical, cultural, biological, and chemical controls to minimize harm to the environment and human health.
definitions.example: IPM is used in agriculture to reduce the use of pesticides and minimize the risk of pest resistance.

definitions.definition: A farming approach that minimizes soil disturbance, maintains soil cover, and promotes crop rotation to reduce soil erosion and promote soil health.
definitions.example: Conservation agriculture is used in many parts of the world to reduce soil erosion and promote soil fertility.

definitions.definition: A farming approach that avoids the use of synthetic fertilizers, pesticides, and genetically modified organisms to promote soil health and biodiversity.
definitions.example: Organic farming is used in many parts of the world to promote soil health and reduce the use of chemicals.

definitions.definition: A holistic approach to managing nutrients that combines organic and inorganic sources to minimize waste and promote soil fertility.
definitions.example: INM is used in agriculture to reduce the use of synthetic fertilizers and promote soil health.

definitions.definition: A farming approach that balances economic, social, and environmental goals to promote long-term productivity and sustainability.
definitions.example: Sustainable agriculture is used in many parts of the world to promote soil health, reduce waste, and minimize the use of chemicals.
